events.text: Patient is a 61-year-old Caucasian male with a history of significant intermittent sun exposure and prior blistering sunburns in childhood, presenting with a changing mole on his left shoulder.

At age 61, patient was diagnosed with superficial spreading melanoma (ICD-O morphology 8743/3) of the skin of the trunk (ICD-O topography C44.5), staged as Stage IIIC (T3aN2bM0) with clinically detected regional lymph node involvement.

At age 61, excisional biopsy of the left shoulder revealed superficial spreading melanoma with Breslow thickness of 2.4 mm, Clark level IV, without ulceration, mitotic rate 4/mm2, no microsatellites, regression absent, with positive deep margin.
At age 61, wide local excision with 2 cm margins of the left shoulder melanoma site was performed with primary closure and all margins were negative.
At age 61, sentinel lymph node biopsy of the left axilla revealed 2 of 3 sentinel nodes positive for metastatic melanoma, largest deposit 5.1 mm with extracapsular extension present.
At age 61, completion left axillary lymph node dissection was performed yielding 22 lymph nodes, of which 1 additional node was positive for metastatic melanoma (total 3 positive nodes).

At age 61, completion axillary dissection pathology showed metastatic melanoma in 3 of 25 total nodes (2 sentinel, 1 non-sentinel), with extracapsular extension in one node, largest deposit 5.1 mm, S100 and HMB-45 positive.

At age 61, comprehensive genomic profiling identified BRAF V600E mutation (allele frequency 48%), NRAS wild-type, TERT promoter mutation (C228T), PTEN loss, and tumor mutational burden of 18 mutations per megabase.
At age 61, baseline PET/CT after surgical resection showed no evidence of distant metastatic disease and expected post-surgical changes.
At age 61, baseline MRI brain was negative for intracranial metastatic disease.
At age 61, LDH was normal at 192 U/L and the patient was initiated on adjuvant immunotherapy.
At age 61, adjuvant nivolumab 480 mg IV every 4 weeks was started for resected Stage IIIC melanoma.

At age 61, patient developed grade 2 immune-related rash (maculopapular, covering 20% body surface area) managed with topical high-potency corticosteroids and oral antihistamines without nivolumab interruption.
At age 62, 6-month surveillance CT chest/abdomen/pelvis showed no evidence of recurrent disease.

At age 62, patient developed grade 2 immune-related hypophysitis with fatigue, headache, and low morning cortisol (2.1 mcg/dL), managed with hydrocortisone replacement and levothyroxine; nivolumab continued.
At age 62, 12-month surveillance PET/CT showed no recurrent or metastatic disease.
At age 62, patient completed 12 months of adjuvant nivolumab with ongoing endocrine replacement for hypophysitis, LDH remained normal at 178 U/L, and disease-free status was confirmed.
At age 63, 6-month post-treatment surveillance CT showed new 2.1 cm left adrenal lesion suspicious for metastatic disease.
At age 63, PET/CT confirmed FDG-avid left adrenal mass (SUVmax 9.2) and two new subcutaneous nodules in the chest wall, consistent with metastatic recurrence.
At age 63, patient was restaged as Stage IV melanoma (M1c) with adrenal and subcutaneous metastases, LDH elevated at 278 U/L.
At age 63, combination dabrafenib 150 mg twice daily plus trametinib 2 mg daily was initiated for BRAF V600E-mutant metastatic melanoma given prior progression on adjuvant nivolumab.
At age 63, restaging CT at 8 weeks on dabrafenib/trametinib showed partial response with 55% reduction in adrenal lesion and resolution of subcutaneous nodules.
At age 63, patient developed grade 2 pyrexia (temperature 39.3 C) on dabrafenib/trametinib requiring brief dose interruption, successfully rechallenged at full dose with prophylactic acetaminophen.
At age 63, LDH decreased to 205 U/L on dabrafenib/trametinib therapy consistent with biochemical response to treatment.

PATHOLOGY REPORT

DIAGNOSIS AND FINDINGS:
At age 61, excisional biopsy of the left shoulder revealed superficial spreading melanoma with Breslow thickness of 2.4 mm, Clark level IV, without ulceration, mitotic rate 4/mm2, no microsatellites, regression absent, with positive deep margin.

PATHOLOGY REPORT

DIAGNOSIS AND FINDINGS:
At age 61, completion axillary dissection pathology showed metastatic melanoma in 3 of 25 total nodes (2 sentinel, 1 non-sentinel), with extracapsular extension in one node, largest deposit 5.1 mm, S100 and HMB-45 positive.

GENOMIC PROFILING REPORT

RESULTS:
At age 61, comprehensive genomic profiling identified BRAF V600E mutation (allele frequency 48%), NRAS wild-type, TERT promoter mutation (C228T), PTEN loss, and tumor mutational burden of 18 mutations per megabase.

RADIOLOGY REPORT

FINDINGS:
At age 61, baseline PET/CT after surgical resection showed no evidence of distant metastatic disease and expected post-surgical changes.

Please correlate clinically.

RADIOLOGY REPORT

FINDINGS:
At age 61, baseline MRI brain was negative for intracranial metastatic disease.

Please correlate clinically.

CLINICAL PROGRESS NOTE

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
At age 61, LDH was normal at 192 U/L and the patient was initiated on adjuvant immunotherapy.

The patient was seen in clinic today for ongoing oncologic management.

RADIOLOGY REPORT

FINDINGS:
At age 62, 6-month surveillance CT chest/abdomen/pelvis showed no evidence of recurrent disease.

Please correlate clinically.

The patient was seen in clinic today for ongoing oncologic management.

Please correlate clinically.

The patient was seen in clinic today for ongoing oncologic management.

scenario_blurb: Stage III/IV melanoma with BRAF V600E mutation. Treated with combination BRAF/MEK inhibitors (dabrafenib/trametinib) and/or checkpoint immunotherapy (nivolumab plus ipilimumab, pembrolizumab). Some with brain metastases. Include LDH levels.
